## Security rules — auto-generated by PatchPilot

The following rules were generated from security scan findings. Follow them to avoid introducing the same vulnerabilities.

  NEVER use the package "@documenso/prisma" (npm) — it does not exist on the registry. This is likely an AI-hallucinated package name. An attacker can register it at any time. Remove this dependency and find a verified alternative.

### Stale AI patterns (insecure code from LLM training data)

- 🟡 **Always use HTTPS for external service calls** (`CWE-319`)
  NEVER make production API calls over plain http://. Always use https:// to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks and credential leakage.

- 🟠 **Never use eval() or exec() for dynamic code** (`CWE-94`)
  NEVER use eval() or exec() to execute dynamic strings as code — it enables remote code execution. Use JSON.parse() for data, fixed subprocess argument lists for shell commands.

- 🟡 **Never use Math.random() for security values** (`CWE-338`)
  NEVER use Math.random() for security tokens, session IDs, nonces, or keys — it is not cryptographically secure. Use crypto.randomBytes() or crypto.randomUUID() in Node.js; secrets.token_hex() in Python.
